summary: This cluster centers on the genetic manipulation of the extremophilic red alga *Cyanidioschyzon merolae*, specifically targeting its chloroplast genome for heterologous protein expression. The research utilized light- and cell cycle-dependent chloroplast promoters to drive stable overexpression of a reporter protein, chloramphenicol acetyltransferase, demonstrating a functional tool for engineering this alga. The findings establish *C. merolae* as a promising chassis for bio-factory applications, leveraging its unique thermophilic and acidophilic physiology. The geographic and ecological scope is implicitly laboratory-based, focusing on the fundamental cellular and plastid biology of this model organism rather than field studies.
